title: Shower Replacement/Remodel

budget.description: Prefab acrylic or fiberglass shower insert, basic valve replacement, standard shower head

midRange.description: Custom tile shower with porcelain tile, new valve and trim, glass shower door, built-in niche

premium.description: Large walk-in shower with natural stone tile, frameless glass enclosure, multiple shower heads, linear drain, bench seat

costFactors.title: Prefab vs. Custom Tile
costFactors.description: A prefab acrylic or fiberglass shower surround costs $1,000-$3,000 installed and can be done in a single day. A custom tile shower with proper waterproofing, cement board, and porcelain tile runs $3,500-$8,000. Premium natural stone tile with frameless glass pushes $8,000-$15,000. This is the single largest cost decision in a shower remodel.

costFactors.title: Shower Enclosure Type
costFactors.description: A shower curtain rod costs $20-$50. A framed glass door runs $300-$800. A semi-frameless door costs $500-$1,200. A frameless glass shower enclosure - the most popular upgrade in mid-range and premium remodels - runs $1,200-$4,000 depending on size, glass thickness, and hardware finish.

costFactors.title: Plumbing Scope
costFactors.description: Replacing just the shower valve and trim kit costs $200-$600. Adding a handheld sprayer or rain shower head adds $150-$500. Installing a full multi-head system with body sprays requires a 3/4-inch supply line and runs $1,000-$3,000. Moving the drain location adds $500-$1,500.

costFactors.title: Shower Size
costFactors.description: A standard 32x32 shower stall uses about 15-18 sq ft of wall and floor area. A 48x36 shower bumps that to 25-30 sq ft. A large walk-in shower (60x36 or bigger) covers 35-50+ sq ft. Every additional square foot of tile adds $15-$35 in materials and labor.

costFactors.title: Tub-to-Shower Conversion
costFactors.description: Converting a tub space to a walk-in shower requires removing the tub, rerouting the drain from the tub position to a shower drain location, adjusting plumbing, and often modifying the subfloor. This adds $1,500-$5,000 compared to a simple shower-for-shower replacement.

materialOptions.pros: Fastest install (1 day), waterproof as a single unit, most affordable
materialOptions.cons: Limited styles, looks budget, can yellow and crack over time
materialOptions.bestFor: Budget remodels, rental properties, quick turnarounds

materialOptions.pros: Highly customizable, durable, wide range of looks, good resale value
materialOptions.cons: Longer install (5-7 days), requires proper waterproofing, grout maintenance

materialOptions.pros: Premium luxury appearance, unique natural patterns, impressive resale impact
materialOptions.cons: Expensive, requires sealing, heavier (may need reinforced substrate), higher maintenance
materialOptions.bestFor: High-end master bathrooms and spa-like retreats

materialOptions.pros: No grout lines, seamless look, easy cleaning, good mid-range option
materialOptions.cons: Limited design options compared to tile, can scratch, repair is harder than tile

materialOptions.pros: Smooth seamless finish, no grout, faster install than tile
materialOptions.cons: Can crack on impact, less durable than solid surface, limited color options
materialOptions.bestFor: Mid-range remodels where you want a clean look without tile maintenance

northeast.label: Northeast
northeast.note: Higher skilled trades labor rates, stricter building codes

westCoast.label: West Coast
westCoast.note: Highest costs nationally, expensive permits in major metros

southeast.label: Southeast
southeast.note: Lower labor costs, strong contractor availability

mountainWest.label: Mountain West
mountainWest.note: Moderate premium, rising with population growth

diyTasks: Demolition and removal of old shower
Installing a shower curtain rod and accessories
Swapping a shower head (no valve work)
proOnlyTasks: Frameless glass door measurement and installation
Building a shower bench or niche
diyVsPro.diyRisks: Shower waterproofing failures are among the most expensive mistakes in home improvement. A leaking shower pan or improperly waterproofed wall sends water into the subfloor and wall framing, causing mold and structural rot that costs $5,000-$15,000+ to remediate. Plumbing valve work in a shower wall requires soldering or PEX connections behind a finished wall - a leak here can cause major damage before you notice it.

savingsTips: Consider a prefab shower insert if your goal is functional, not luxury. A quality acrylic unit runs $1,000-$3,000 installed vs. $3,500-$7,000 for custom tile.
Keep the shower in its current footprint. Expanding the shower or moving the drain adds $1,500-$5,000 in plumbing and framing work.
Choose a semi-frameless glass door over fully frameless. Semi-frameless doors cost $500-$1,200 installed vs. $1,200-$4,000 for frameless and still look great.
Use porcelain tile instead of natural stone. Porcelain that mimics marble or travertine costs $4-$10/sq ft vs. $15-$35 for real stone.
Skip the body spray system. A rain shower head with a handheld sprayer delivers a spa feel for $200-$500 vs. $1,000-$3,000 for a multi-head system that also requires upgraded water supply.
Do your own demolition. Tearing out an old shower surround saves $300-$800 in labor. Just be careful with the plumbing behind the wall.
Build a tile-ready foam niche instead of a custom one. Pre-formed shower niches cost $30-$80 and save significant framing and tiling labor.
Time your remodel for the off-season. Contractors are busiest in spring and summer. Fall and winter remodels may come with 5-15% discounts.

contractorQuestions.question: What waterproofing system do you use for the shower pan and walls?
contractorQuestions.why: The answer should name a specific system like Schluter Kerdi, Laticrete Hydro Ban, or a hot-mopped pan. A vague answer is a serious red flag for a shower project.

contractorQuestions.question: Have you done tub-to-shower conversions before, and can I see examples?
contractorQuestions.why: Conversions involve drain relocation, subfloor modification, and often framing changes. You want someone with specific experience, not a general handyman.

contractorQuestions.question: What is included in your quote - just the shower or also the glass door?
contractorQuestions.why: Some tile contractors do not install glass doors, requiring a separate glass company. Make sure you know exactly what is and is not covered.

contractorQuestions.question: How do you slope the shower floor for drainage?
contractorQuestions.why: A properly sloped shower floor is critical to prevent standing water. The standard is 1/4 inch per foot toward the drain. This question tests their attention to detail.

contractorQuestions.question: Will the shower valve brand you install have replacement parts available long-term?
contractorQuestions.why: Cheap or off-brand valves can be impossible to get cartridges for in 5-10 years. Brands like Moen, Delta, and Kohler have reliable long-term parts availability.

contractorQuestions.question: Do you pull permits for shower remodels?
contractorQuestions.why: Most areas require permits for plumbing work. Unpermitted work can cause problems when selling your home and may void your insurance coverage.

contractorQuestions.question: What is your timeline, and will I be without a shower during the project?
contractorQuestions.why: A tile shower takes 5-10 days minimum. If this is your only shower, you need to plan accordingly. Some contractors can stage the work to minimize downtime.

seo.metaDescription: How much does a shower remodel cost in 2026? National average is $5,500. Compare prefab vs. custom tile costs, get a free calculator, and learn what drives the price up or down.
